claims.claim: Structural risk-on — long-horizon, builder-focused, constructive backdrop
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Built out an extensive 'tokencraft' framework — token taxonomy (memecoin/stablecoin/arcade/network), progressive decentralization, faucets & sinks, paying for security — that the prediction never anticipated
Deep DAO-governance focus: dissecting the Compound 'governance attack,' timing-game/permissionless-governance design, and promoting the DUNA legal framework
Hands-on ZK and formal-verification tooling as priorities: Halmos symbolic testing, Jolt/Nexus zkVMs, and Helios light clients for L2 interoperability
Vocal open-source AI advocacy (defending open-weight models, arguing they cause no identifiable harm) — beyond the predicted AI×crypto framing
Crypto-user-measurement methodology: critiquing 'active addresses' and publishing a 30–60M real-monthly-user estimate
notes: Eddy's window output strongly confirms his AI×crypto thesis (esp. proof-of-personhood/Sybil resistance, AI-agent wallets, agent-to-agent and compute payments), stablecoins/payments PMF, Ethereum L2 scaling (blob-target push, Helios), decentralized social (Farcaster), and his dismissive memecoin stance — all in his own voice plus the a16z State of Crypto report he co-authored. Risk posture stays structurally constructive and builder-focused, never bearish, exactly as the structural risk-on call described. No contradictions found. The three 'absent' claims are not personally addressed: DePIN appears only in colleagues' Big Ideas entries (Carra Wu), not Eddy's; the low-float/high-FDV critique and restaking/leverage-on-security concern are never raised, though his token-launch talk touches adjacent themes (lockups, market maturity, security budgets). His biggest unanticipated emphases are token-design craft, DAO governance, and ZK/verification tooling.
